title: Algorithmic Problems, Contracts, and Evidence
description: Turn vague routing and lookup requests into measurable input-output problems. Learners cumulatively produce an algorithm contract and adversarial case catalog through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Specify inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, and invalid cases before selecting an algorithm.
State partial and total correctness claims that can be challenged by counterexamples.
Construct normal, boundary, duplicate, empty, sorted, reversed, and adversarial cases.
